notes: All CWV thresholds match Google's official definitions (measured at p75 of field data). INP replaced FID as the official responsiveness CWV in March 2024. LCP/CLS/INP are the three confirmed ranking signals. FCP and TTFB are diagnostic only — not direct ranking factors, but leading indicators of LCP issues. Title and description max-char limits are deliberately loose: Google's SERP snippet renders only ~60 title / ~160 description chars in the typical pixel budget, but the full string still feeds ranking + AI search citation, so we only flag titles >100 chars and descriptions >200 chars. Anything within the SERP budget passes silently; anything past it is reported as an info/warning suggestion, not a hard fail. To update this file: research current Google CWV thresholds at web.dev/articles/vitals and update the numeric values. The notes field should explain what changed and why.
